title: lathyrus lanszwertii var. sandbergii
intro: Lathyrus lanszwertii var. sandbergii, also known as Sandberg's pea or Sandberg's sweet pea, is a perennial legume plant native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of isoleucine, a non-protein amino acid. The plant's health benefits include potential uses in treating various conditions, such as arthritis, gout, and cardiovascular disease, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, Lathyrus lanszwertii var. sandbergii has been prepared as a tea or decoction for its medicinal uses, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
